description_long: This is a soprano recorder-style flute, typically made from beige or light brown plastic. The body is cylindrical and features a series of evenly spaced finger holes along its length, with a slightly flared bell at one end and a beaked mouthpiece at the other. The flute has subtle molded rings and ridges for grip and decorative effect, and the finger holes are clearly defined and symmetrically aligned. The mouthpiece is a fipple design, characteristic of recorders, allowing for easy sound production. The surface appears smooth and slightly glossy, with no visible engravings or embellishments. The overall construction suggests a lightweight, mass-produced instrument suitable for educational or casual urban use.
description: A beige plastic soprano recorder flute with a fipple mouthpiece, flared bell, and smooth, glossy surface. It features evenly spaced finger holes and molded decorative rings.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flared bell and aligned finger holes along the body.
description_view_1: Side profile highlighting the cylindrical shape, finger holes, and beaked mouthpiece.
description_view_2: Rear view focusing on the bell end and the back of the flute, with less detail on the finger holes.
description_view_3: Opposite side profile showing the full length, finger holes, and mouthpiece design.
